Just now

--

Angular isn’t just evolving — it has fundamentally changed how we build modern web applications. If you’re still writing Angular the same way you did a few years ago, you’re probably missing out on significant improvements in performance, developer experience, and application architecture.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

10 things every Angular developer should know in 2026Over the last few releases, Angular has introduced features that reduce boilerplate, improve runtime performance, simplify state management, and make applications easier to scale.
In this article, I’ll walk through 10 essential Angular concepts every developer should know in 2026. Whether you’re building enterprise applications or preparing for your next interview, these features should be part of your daily toolkit.
1. Stop Writing Angular Like It’s 2020
Angular has evolved significantly.
Many projects still rely on patterns that were considered best practices years ago:

• NgModules everywhere

• Constructor injection for every dependency

• *ngIf and *ngFor

• Large eagerly-loaded modules

• Zone.js-driven change detection
Modern Angular encourages a different approach:

• Standalone Components

• Signals

• Built-in Control Flow

• Functional Dependency Injection

• Lazy Loading by Default

• Zoneless Change Detection
The result is cleaner code, better performance, and a much better developer experience.
